hypothesis:
Based on the paper's findings that LoQI can identify lower-energy conformers than those in the ChEMBL3D training dataset (in 14% of test cases), and that the model learns energy landscapes from implicit solvent CPCM-AIMNet2 optimizations, I propose the following hypothesis:

**Hypothesis:** Training LoQI on conformer ensembles generated under explicit water solvation conditions will enable the model to predict protein-ligand binding poses with higher accuracy than the current implicit solvent-trained model, as explicit solvation better captures the discrete water-mediated interactions that stabilize bound conformations in crystal structures.
